response.justification: Candidate is a highly experienced senior/staff engineer with 15+ years of experience, applying to an entry-level Junior Java Developer role. This represents a severe seniority mismatch — overqualification typically leads to retention risk, compensation misalignment, and likely disengagement. Additionally, German proficiency is only Basic (role requires strong verbal and written German), and Java is a minor part of the candidate's stack (primarily TypeScript, Node.js, DLang). Insurance domain experience is absent.
strengths: Deep software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, agile, pairing, trunk-based development
Strong full-stack and architecture experience across multiple languages including Java
Proven track record of knowledge sharing (workshops, talks, courses) — useful for team mentoring
concerns: Massive seniority mismatch — Staff/Senior engineer applying for entry-level role
German is only Basic; role requires strong written and verbal German
Java is peripheral to candidate's actual stack; primary expertise is TS/Node/DLang, no insurance domain exposure

key_factors.factor: Strong engineering fundamentals and agile experience
